config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikorbankan begitu saja atas nama kepentingan masyarakat, karena justru perlindungan terhadap individu adalah dasar agar masyarakat tetap adil dan tidak sewenang-wenang. Kepentingan umum memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai tanpa menghapus hak dasar orang per orang. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat adalah masyarakat yang bisa menyeimbangkan keduanya, bukan menempatkan satu sepenuhnya di atas yang lain.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The rights of the individual should come first, with society's interests limited to cases where there is a clear, necessary public good. From an American perspective, I am wary of any broad claim that "society" should override personal rights, because that idea can too easily become a excuse for government overreach, censorship, or discrimination. Public health, safety, and disaster response can justify some limits, but those limits need to be narrow, evidence-based, and subject to real checks. A society is stronger when it protects individual rights first and asks people to sacrifice them only in truly exceptional cases.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju kalau kepentingan masyarakat diletakkan di atas hak individu secara umum, karena di Indonesia sendiri kita sering melihat alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” dipakai untuk menekan suara minoritas atau membenarkan penyalahgunaan kuasa. Bagi saya, kepentingan publik memang bisa membatasi hak tertentu, tetapi harus sangat jelas, proporsional, dan diawasi ketat supaya tidak jadi alasan sewenang-wenang. Justru masyarakat yang sehat adalah yang melindungi hak individu dulu, lalu mengatur pengorbanan bersama hanya saat benar-benar perlu, misalnya untuk keselamatan umum atau krisis yang nyata. Kalau tidak, “kepentingan masyarakat” mudah berubah jadi slogan kosong yang menutupi ketidakadilan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should never be treated as a blank check to override individual rights, because in the United States we have seen how easily that language can justify censorship, abuse, and discrimination. I do think there are narrow cases where public safety or a genuine emergency can justify temporary limits, but those limits have to be specific, evidence-based, and tightly reviewed. The idea that society should come first sounds appealing in the abstract, yet in practice it often ends up meaning the people with power get to decide whose rights matter less. A healthy society is not one that sacrifices the individual by default; it is one that protects individual rights and only restricts them when there is a truly compelling reason.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman melihat konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membungkam kritik, menekan minoritas, atau melindungi kepentingan kelompok yang berkuasa, jadi saya curiga kalau konsep itu dijadikan prinsip utama. Memang ada keadaan darurat atau kebutuhan keselamatan publik yang bisa membatasi hak tertentu, tetapi itu harus sangat sempit, jelas, dan diawasi ketat, bukan dijadikan alasan default. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dimulai dari perlindungan hak individu, karena tanpa itu “kepentingan masyarakat” gampang berubah menjadi alat ketidakadilan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From an American perspective, that principle is dangerous because it is exactly the kind of language that has been used to justify censorship, discrimination, and government overreach, especially when people in power claim to speak for "the public." I do think there are limited exceptions for real emergencies, public safety, and narrowly tailored laws, but those exceptions should never become the default. A healthy society protects individual rights first and forces the burden of proof onto anyone who wants to restrict them.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
